label: Proton beams / differential flow (Alterman_2018_ApJ_864_112)
source_note: Content recovered from blocked agent return message; agent followed spec voice rules with paper-writer integration. Drafts not re-authored.

patches.description: A three-population fit to Wind Faraday cup spectra separates proton cores, proton beams, and alpha particles, unlocking 20+ years of archival data for simultaneous differential-flow comparisons.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary_short field; paper abstract; spec figure-detail voice.

patches.description: The proton beam shows up as a field-aligned shoulder extending from the proton core in velocity space, cleanly separated from the alpha distribution and confirming the three-population fit is not an artifact.
In fast, nearly collisionless solar wind, proton beams drift at 108% of the Alfven speed while alpha particles drift at 67%, with the alpha distribution noticeably broader.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary.the_finding; paper abstract drift-speed values; quantitative emphasis per paper-writer.

patches.description: Minute-scale fluctuations in alpha and proton-beam drift speeds are uncorrelated -- the 2D distribution is a circle, ruling out a common large-scale wave driving both species in tandem.
Alpha drift decays from 80% to 40% of the Alfven speed as collisional age increases, while proton beam drift stays flat near the Alfven speed -- only the alphas feel Coulomb friction.
In the youngest measured plasma, alpha particles drift at 71% of the proton beam speed, up from 62% over the broader collisionless range, and the ratio distribution narrows by 27%.
Normalizing by an Alfven speed that includes proton core temperature anisotropy tightens the drift distributions to widths of 0.132 for alphas and 0.150 for beams, with flat residuals.

The anisotropic Alfven speed produces the narrowest drift distributions for both alphas and beams; adding the dynamic pressure correction broadens them, indicating that term carries more noise than signal.

Extrapolated to zero Coulomb collisions, alpha particles drift at 67% of the Alfven speed and proton beams drift at 105% -- evidence the two populations launch from the corona with fundamentally different drift speeds.
patches.voice_grounding_source: summary.the_finding; paper abstract zero-collision values; quantitative emphasis.
